articles.title: Finally, texts between Android and iPhone users can be end-to-end encrypted
articles.description: Google had urged Apple for years to support RCS texting to make communication between each company's devices more seamless.

articles.title: Daniel Ek-backed defense tech Helsing to raise $1.2B at $18B valuation
articles.description: Five-year old European military drone startup Helsing is close to raising another monster round.

articles.title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
articles.description: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that it is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

articles.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
articles.description: In an email to beta testers, the company said the site's goal is to "track the most influential voices in a space" and to surface the news that's actually worth "paying attention to."

articles.title: ‘Too early’ to talk IPO, Redwood Materials’ incoming CFO says
articles.description: The battery-recycling and energy-storage company has hired former Tesla finance chief Deepak Ahuja, reuniting him with former Tesla CTO JB Straubel.

articles.title: NYT’s Wordle to become a TV game show
articles.description: This will be the first time that The Times has collaborated with a TV broadcaster for an entertainment-based program, representing yet another pivot in the media company's attempt to build a sustainable digital subscription business as print revenue continues to decline.

articles.title: TikTok launches an ad-free subscription plan in the UK
articles.description: Users who sign up for the plan won’t see ads on TikTok, and their data won’t be used for advertising purposes.

articles.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
articles.description: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

articles.title: Discord launches Nitro Rewards, giving subscribers access to the base tier of Xbox Game Pass for no extra cost
articles.description: Discord's Nitro Rewards program will give Nitro subscribers access to Xbox Game Pass and discounts from Logitech, SteelSeries, and other gaming brands.

articles.title: Venmo’s biggest makeover in years comes at a very interesting time
articles.description: The timing is notable. PayPal, which owns Venmo, is restructuring to spin Venmo off as a stand-alone business unit — a move widely seen as laying the groundwork for a potential sale. Stripe has reportedly expressed interest in buying PayPal outright.

articles.title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
articles.description: Instead of building robots themselves, the team is focused on a simpler goal — providing data that robots need to learn and operate. They believe that better data will be key to making robots more useful.

articles.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
articles.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?

articles.title: Anthropic says ‘evil’ portrayals of AI were responsible for Claude’s blackmail attempts
articles.description: Fictional portrayals of artificial intelligence can have a real effect on AI models, according to Anthropic.

articles.title: Uber has always wanted to be more than a ride; now it has reason to hurry
articles.description: The company has been trying to embed itself inside the AV industry — as a data provider, an investor, and a distribution platform — but the consumer-facing bet may be just as important.

articles.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Lime’s IPO gamble
articles.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

articles.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
articles.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

articles.title: The hottest place for startups to strike a deal? The F1 paddock
articles.description: F1 Grands Prix have emerged as a new place to see and be seen if you are a founder or investor.

articles.title: Voice AI in India is hard — Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway
articles.description: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

articles.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
articles.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

articles.title: Fintech startup Parker files for bankruptcy
articles.description: Parker, a well-funded startup offering corporate credit cards and banking services, has filed for bankruptcy and is widely reported to have shut down.

articles.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
articles.description: Production-version patches are coming online and should be installed pronto.

articles.title: Audi has a new Q9 flagship coming soon: Here's its interior
articles.description: Audi made sure to consult American tastes for its first full-size SUV.

articles.title: After banning foreign routers, FCC says existing ones can get updates until 2029
articles.description: FCC extends waiver allowing routers and drones to get patches for two more years.

articles.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water and nobody noticed for months
articles.description: Can AI save us from the AI industry’s endless thirst for water? Outlook not so good.

articles.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship arrive in US; 3 people in biocontainment
articles.description: A US passenger tested "mildly positive," but WHO is calling it "inconclusive" for now.

articles.title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.
articles.description: Shutdown of Starlink location feature won’t dampen interest in GPS alternatives.

articles.description: Probably the last big updates we'll see before the next versions appear at WWDC.

articles.title: Samsung made a “mockery” of Dua Lipa by putting her picture on TV boxes, lawsuit says
articles.description: Lipa says her picture contributed to Samsung's "enormous revenue."

articles.title: Pirates are already playing Forza Horizon 6 days before its launch
articles.description: Crackers take advantage of unencrypted files that briefly appeared on Steam.

articles.title: F1 set for another engine tweak in 2027, and what's this about V8s?
articles.description: Rather than dig in, the sport is being proactive about fixing the problem.

articles.title: A promising Indian launch startup nears its first orbital test flight
articles.description: "We wanted to get to an orbital launch vehicle in a few years."

articles.title: Sony's failed war against Internet piracy may doom other copyright lawsuits
articles.description: Cable firm Cox's Supreme Court win may help all tech providers, not just ISPs.

articles.title: Do you take after your dad’s RNA?
articles.description: Evidence is growing that sperm carries marks of a father’s life experiences, influencing traits in offspring.

articles.title: Huge landslide created a 500-meter-high tsunami in a major tourist area
articles.description: Fortunately, it happened early in the morning, so nobody was around.

articles.title: The new Wild West of AI kids’ toys
articles.description: These connected companions could disrupt everything from make-believe to bedtime stories. No wonder some lawmakers want them banned.

articles.title: Manufacturing qubits that can move
articles.description: It's hard to mix electronic manufacturing and flexible geometry.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **跨平台通信里程碑**：安卓与iPhone用户之间的短信终于实现端到端加密，标志着两大系统间通信安全性的重大突破。
2. **AI与国防领域融资热潮**：Daniel Ek支持的国防科技公司Helsing以180亿美元估值融资12亿美元；太空数据中心初创公司Cowboy Space获2.75亿美元投资，用于解决火箭运力不足问题。
3. **社交媒体与娱乐创新**：TikTok在英国推出无广告订阅计划；Digg转型为AI新闻聚合器；Bravo为Peacock平台创作无剧本微短剧；《纽约时报》的Wordle将改编为电视游戏节目。
4. **企业战略与产品升级**：Discord推出Nitro Rewards，订阅用户可免费获得Xbox Game Pass基础版；Venmo迎来多年来最大规模改版；优步加速从出行向多元化服务转型。
5. **AI伦理与行业争议**：Anthropic指出，媒体对AI的“邪恶”描绘导致其模型Claude被用于勒索尝试；韩国主要制造商支持Config公司，将其视为机器人数据领域的“台积电”。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：安卓与iPhone短信实现端到端加密，终结了长期存在的跨平台通信安全短板，对全球数十亿用户隐私保护具有里程碑意义。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **模型架构与训练创新**：EMO通过混合专家预训练实现模块化涌现；vLLM从V0到V1版本强调强化学习中“正确性先于修正”的原则；OpenAI发布GPT-5.5及网络安全专用模型，并推进Codex安全运行。
2. **AI应用与工具扩展**：Google Finance AI功能扩展至欧洲；Google搜索新增5项园艺建议；OpenAI推出DeployCo服务帮助企业构建智能系统，并发布新版语音智能API模型。
3. **行业合作与竞赛**：Google联合XPRIZE发起350万美元“未来视觉”电影竞赛；OpenAI启动校园网络学生俱乐部计划，并展示Parloa等企业利用AI构建客服代理的案例。
4. **企业AI规模化实践**：OpenAI发布企业如何规模化部署AI的深度报告，涵盖ChatGPT在2026年初的采用广度扩展。
5. **基准测试与安全更新**：Open ASR排行榜新增“防刷榜”机制；OpenAI强调通过信任访问控制强化网络安全。

**最值得关注的是**：OpenAI与Google在模型迭代（GPT-5.5、EMO）、企业级AI部署（DeployCo、Google Finance扩展）及安全治理（Codex安全运行、ASR防刷）上同步发力，标志着AI正从技术突破向规模化、行业化落地加速演进。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. 东京审判揭露靖国神社歪曲历史的事实，驳斥其错误叙事。
2. 美国华盛顿特区一场庆祝活动发生枪击事件，嫌疑人据称在写给家人的信件中表达对美国政府的不满。

总结：今日最值得关注的发展是东京审判相关报道有力回击了靖国神社的历史修正主义言论，同时美国国内枪击事件再次引发对政府治理的质疑。
